palette: Polished charcoal, heritage brick red, and industrial brass, with a cold Toronto-lake-mist overlay
lighting: Soft, diffused morning light through a large factory-style industrial window; a slight blue-hour tint remains in the shadows
instagram_prompts: A tight, high-angle macro shot of a wet, dark gray cobblestone street in Toronto's Old Town. A single, discarded Blue Jays baseball ticket is flattened against the damp stone. Cold, morning light glints off the water in the cracks. First-person perspective (FPP), world feels enormous.

First-person perspective (FPP) looking out from an industrial, black-framed window at the The Ivy at Verity. The view is a tight, vertical slice of a red brick courtyard with ivy clinging to the walls, partially obscured by morning mist. A small, brass desk clock in the foreground is out of focus. World is impossibly large.

A close-up, first-person perspective (FPP) of a sleek, brushed-aluminum laptop lid covered in small, colorful tech stickers (DigitalOcean shark, a Toronto 'TO' icon, and a 'sudo' sticker). The laptop sits on a heavy, dark wood table next to a steaming porcelain cup of black coffee. The steam catches the soft, cold morning light. No text or overlays.
